suppress.notes: False positive: CVE-2021-34364 is for "Refined GitHub" (browser extension), not io.circe:circe-refined_3
file name: elasticsearch-rest-client-7.10.2.jar - provided dependency, rest-client is used to send only predefined requests (audit)
file name: elasticsearch-rest-client-X.Y.Z.jar - The CVEs affect the org.elasticsearch:elasticsearch package, which is not a dependency of elasticsearch-rest-client
file name: transport-netty4-client-X.Y.Z.jar - The CVEs affect the org.elasticsearch:elasticsearch package, which is not a dependency of transport-netty4-client
file name: bcpkix-fips-1.0.7.jar // it's a false positive https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-72m5-fvvv-55m6 https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wjxj-5m7g-mg7q
file name: bctls-fips-1.0.19.jar // it's a false positive https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-72m5-fvvv-55m6 https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wjxj-5m7g-mg7q

False positive: CVE-2026-42582 affects only netty-codec-http3 (HTTP/3 QPACK decoder) in Netty 4.2.x. The project uses Netty 4.1.x, which does not include netty-codec-http3 at all — the module does not exist in the 4.1.x line. The flagged netty artifacts (netty-buffer, netty-codec, netty-codec-http, netty-codec-http2, netty-common, netty-handler, netty-resolver, netty-transport, netty-transport-native-unix-common) are unaffected.

False positive: CVE-2026-54399 and CVE-2026-54428 affect the HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 parsers in the rewritten Apache HttpComponents Core 5.x line (org.apache.httpcomponents.core5:httpcore5 / httpcore5-h2, package org.apache.hc.core5). The CVEs' CPE match criteria (cpe:2.3:a:apache:httpcomponents_core:*, versionEndIncluding 5.4.2, no versionStartIncluding) has no lower bound, so it also matches the unrelated legacy 4.x line (org.apache.httpcomponents:httpcore / httpcore-nio, package org.apache.http), which is a completely different, unaffected codebase used by httpclient:4.5.14 and elasticsearch-rest-client in the es*x modules.

file name: log4j-api-2.11.2.jar - CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell): requires log4j-core's JNDI lookup; log4j-core is provided by Elasticsearch at runtime — must be fixed by Elasticsearch upgrading to log4j-core:2.25.4+ - CVE-2021-45046 (Log4Shell): requires log4j-core's JNDI lookup; same as above - CVE-2021-44832 (Log4Shell): requires log4j-core's JNDI lookup; same as above - CVE-2021-45105 (Log4Shell): requires log4j-core's JNDI lookup; same as above - CVE-2020-9488: affects log4j-core's SMTP appender; same as above — log4j-core is not bundled by the plugin - CVE-2025-68161: affects log4j-core's Socket Appender (TLS hostname verification); same as above - CVE-2026-34480: affects log4j-core's XmlLayout; same as above - CVE-2026-34479: affects log4j-1.2-api (Log4j 1-to-2 bridge) Log4j1XmlLayout; false positive for log4j-api — log4j-1.2-api is used only in tests in an already patched version Note: upgrading log4j-api to 2.25.4 without matching log4j-core causes runtime compatibility issues.

file name: log4j-slf4j-impl-2.11.2.jar - CVE-2020-9488 affects log4j-core's SMTP appender; log4j-core is excluded from this dependency (see core/build.gradle) - CVE-2026-34479 affects log4j-1.2-api (Log4j 1-to-2 bridge) Log4j1XmlLayout; log4j-slf4j-impl does not use Log4j1XmlLayout — false positive

file name: scala-parallel-collections_3-1.2.0.jar False positive: CVE-2017-1000034 is for akka:akka, CPE matcher incorrectly matches version 1.2.0 to akka:akka:1.2.0. scala-parallel-collections is an unrelated Scala standard library module.
